name: Phantom Types
def: Type parameters that carry compile-time information without appearing in the runtime representation.
problem: Encode states/units/permissions in the type so misuse is a compile error, at zero runtime cost.
mechanism: A generic parameter (e.g. Meter vs Foot, Validated vs Raw) is used only by the type checker; it influences which operations are allowed.
benefits: Catch unit/state mix-ups at compile time
limits: Limited expressiveness vs full dependent types
whenNot: When a plain newtype or runtime check suffices
myths: "Adds runtime cost" — phantom parameters are erased; zero cost
